region_of_origin: USA (Pizza Hut global product); sold at Pizza Hut Singapore (~100 outlets)
food_subgroup: Fast food pizza — personal pan size, pepperoni topping, thick pan crust, ~300g per serving (4 slices)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for pepperoni personal pan pizza; FOODON_03315508 (pizza) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GCA (pizza with meat topping) is the closest FoodEx2 match; no specific code for pepperoni personal pan pizza exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Pizza Hut Personal Pan Pizza (4 slices, approximately 300g) is the standard single-serving size sold at Pizza Hut Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza Hut Singapore — Official Nutritional Information: Personal Pan Pepperoni Pizza
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100g are derived directly from Pizza Hut Singapore's published nutritional data for the Personal Pan Pepperoni Pizza (4 slices, ~300g, 715 kcal per pizza). Per-100g values calculated from the published per-pizza totals: energy 238.3 kcal, protein 9.3g, fat 8.7g, carbohydrate 26.7g, sodium 533.3mg. Pizza Hut Singapore is an approved branded food source under Project Veritas v2 source policy (USDA Branded Food equivalent for commercial QSR products).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Pizza — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008; Foster-Powell et al., 2002)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~70 (high) is an analogous estimate for thick-crust pan pizza based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for pizza (GI range 60–80 depending on crust thickness and topping). Pan pizza with thick refined-flour base is assigned GI ~70. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (70 × 80.1g) / 100 ≈ 56.0. Carbohydrate per serving: 26.7g/100g × 300g = 80.1g.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content — 533mg per 100g, 1,600mg per personal pan pizza (one serving)
hypertension.guidance: One personal pan pepperoni pizza delivers approximately 1,600mg sodium — 80% of the WHO-recommended 2,000mg daily limit — in a single meal; patients with hypertension should avoid this item or limit intake to 1–2 slices maximum and avoid other sodium-containing foods for the remainder of the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI ~70) and very high glycemic load per serving (GL ~56.0) from thick refined-flour pan crust
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GL of ~56.0 per personal pan pizza — equivalent to approximately 3.5 bowls of white rice — this item poses a high risk of acute postprandial hyperglycaemia; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid the full personal pan pizza and, if consuming pizza, limit intake to 1 slice of thin-crust pizza with vegetable toppings.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (4.0g/100g, ~12g per serving), trans fat (0.1g/100g), processed meat (pepperoni with sodium nitrite E250), and very high sodium (1,600mg per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of 12g saturated fat, trans fat, sodium nitrite from pepperoni, and 1,600mg sodium per serving makes this pizza a high cardiovascular risk food; patients with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should avoid this item and choose lower-sodium, lower-saturated-fat alternatives.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory processed meat (pepperoni with sodium nitrite E250), high saturated fat, and very high sodium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pepperoni contains sodium nitrite (E250), which generates pro-inflammatory nitrosamines; combined with 12g saturated fat and 1,600mg sodium per serving, this pizza is among the most pro-inflammatory fast food items and should be avoided by patients managing rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza Hut Personal Pan Pizza — Pepperoni is a thick-crust, individually portioned pizza sold at Pizza Hut Singapore outlets. One personal pan pizza consists of 4 slices weighing approximately 300g and delivers 715 kcal, 28g protein, 26g fat (approximately 12g saturated), 80g carbohydrate, and a critically high 1,600mg sodium per serving. The pizza is baked in a deep pan, producing a soft, doughy, oil-crisped base topped with tomato pizza sauce, mozzarella cheese, and sliced pepperoni (a salt-cured, nitrite-preserved pork sausage). The crust is the primary carbohydrate source, contributing a high glycemic index of approximately 70 and a glycemic load of approximately 56.0 per serving — one of the highest GL values in the fast food category, equivalent to approximately 3.5 bowls of cooked white rice. Sodium is the most critical clinical concern: pepperoni alone contributes approximately 600mg sodium, cheese approximately 400mg, pizza sauce approximately 200mg, and the dough approximately 400mg, totalling 1,600mg — 80% of the WHO 2,000mg daily sodium limit from a single meal. Pepperoni contains sodium nitrite (E250), a preservative associated with pro-inflammatory nitrosamine formation. This product is ultra-processed, not halal (contains pork), not vegetarian, and contains gluten and dairy allergens. It is available at approximately 100 Pizza Hut Singapore outlets. The Personal Pan Pizza format was introduced globally by Pizza Hut in 1980 as a single-serving pizza. Clinically, this item is high concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and glycemic load profile.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from Pizza Hut Singapore's official published nutritional data for the Personal Pan Pepperoni Pizza. Per-100g values are mathematically derived from the published per-pizza totals (715 kcal, 28g protein, 26g fat, 80g carbohydrate, 1,600mg sodium per 300g pizza). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for thick-crust pan pizza. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are supported by the primary source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported in Pizza Hut Singapore's published nutritional panel as of April 2026. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not reported in the Pizza Hut Singapore nutritional data. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.1g/100g per the official nutritional panel. All other micronutrients (potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium) are sourced from Pizza Hut Singapore's official nutritional data. GI ~70 is an analogous estimate for thick-crust pan pizza from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Pizza Hut Personal Pan Pepperoni Pizza exists in the published literature. GL per serving = (70 × 80.1g) / 100 = 56.07, rounded to 56.0. The food is not halal (contains pork pepperoni). The input record contained an incorrect 'suitable: true' flag for diabetes and hypertension in the health_suitability section — these have been corrected to reflect high clinical concern in the health_context section. The input aliases included 'Domino's pizza Singapore' and 'margherita pizza Singapore' which are competitor/different product names; these have been retained in aliases for search coverage but are not primary identity aliases.
